Through the Jambu fruits (Rose-apples) the growth of sesa-
mum and black gram is ascertained; through the excellent growth

Prognostics from Flowers and Creepers XXIX 283
of Sirlsa the success of Priyahgu (panic seed) is assessed; through
the Madhuka trees, of wheat, and through the Saptaparna, of
barley.

The prospects of cotton crop are to be declared from the
Atimuktaka tree and the Kunda jasmine flowers; those of mustard,
from the Asana trees; those of horse-grara, from the jujube fruits;
and those of green gram, from the Cirabilva (black silk-cotton).
wcmt ^n: i
\\\\\
Atasi (Unseed) is to be determined from the flowers of the
Vetasa tree; Kadrava (a kind of grain), from the flowers of the Pafafa
tree; conch shells, pearls and silver, from the Tilaka tree; and hemp,
